confidence_note: Confidence levels: HIGH (strong evidence from binary analysis), MEDIUM (moderate evidence), LOW (weak evidence). All hashes confirmed from triage data. Network IOCs extracted from hardcoded configuration in analyzed binaries.
data_gaps: main.exe (XiebroC2) SHA256 hash not captured in triage artifacts provided — obtain from infrastructure analyst or original retrieval
s.d IP 192.168.1.100 excluded — RFC 1918 placeholder/test value, not a live C2

ipv4.context: XiebroC2 v3.1 TCP C2 — hardcoded in main.exe binary; AES-128-ECB encrypted binary protocol; 4-byte LE length prefix framing; jittered 0-5 second reconnect beacon
Covenant C2 HTTP listener — cleartext HTTP on port 443 (not HTTPS); both GruntStager builds connect here for three-phase key exchange and Grunt payload delivery
Open directory staging server — all four malicious samples hosted here; HTTP file serving

urls.context: Covenant stager probe GET — random path selected from pool; masquerades as Microsoft Docs URL
Covenant stager C2 POST path — random path selected from pool
Covenant stager C2 POST path — random path selected from pool

user_agents.ua: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/41.0.2228.0 Safari/537.36
user_agents.context: Hardcoded User-Agent in both Covenant GruntStager builds — Chrome 41 on Windows 7; present in every HTTP request from either stager

network_patterns.pattern: HTTP POST to 193.56.255.154:443 with body matching: i=a19ea23062db990386a3a478cb89d52e&data=[base64]&session=75db-99b1-25fe4e9afbe58696-320bea73
network_patterns.context: Covenant stager C2 POST pattern — listener-level constants appear in every request from both GruntStager builds; highest-value network detection

network_patterns.pattern: HTTP response body from 193.56.255.154:443 containing: // Hello World!
network_patterns.context: Covenant C2 data wrapper in HTML response body — C2 payload embedded in HTML comment; detectable by proxy/IDS with response body inspection

network_patterns.pattern: TCP connection to 193.56.255.154:4444 — binary 4-byte LE length prefix framing, AES-128-ECB payload
network_patterns.context: XiebroC2 v3.1 TCP C2 wire protocol; random 0-5 second reconnect jitter; 50KB chunked writes
Covenant HTTP (not HTTPS) on standard HTTPS port — anomalous cleartext on port 443; detectable by protocol inspection

process_patterns.pattern: main.exe (Go binary) loading mscoree.dll or clr.dll at runtime
process_patterns.context: XiebroC2 inline-assembly command: go-clr loads CLR into Go process — no legitimate Go binary performs this; Sysmon Event ID 7

process_patterns.pattern: main.exe spawning cmd.exe or powershell.exe child processes with HideWindow=true
process_patterns.context: XiebroC2 shell/OSshell/OSpowershell commands — all spawn with CREATE_NO_WINDOW flag; Sysmon Event ID 1

process_patterns.pattern: Non-.NET process followed by ETW DotNETRuntime AssemblyLoad (Event ID 152)
process_patterns.context: Fileless .NET assembly loading — covers Covenant Assembly.Load() and XiebroC2 go-clr CLR hosting; fires regardless of disk artifacts

process_patterns.pattern: VirtualAllocEx + WriteProcessMemory + VirtualProtectEx (RW to RX) + CreateRemoteThreadEx sequence from main.exe to target process
process_patterns.context: XiebroC2 Migration command: shellcode injection via RunCreateRemoteThread; all four APIs dynamically resolved via LazyProc

process_patterns.pattern: CreateProcess with CREATE_SUSPENDED=0x4 followed immediately by NtQueryInformationProcess and ReadProcessMemory on child process from main.exe
process_patterns.context: XiebroC2 process hollowing (RunCreateProcessWithPipe/RunPE): entry point patching injection pattern; unique PE parser API sequence

crypto_artifacts.note: Crypto artifacts are high-value IOCs enabling retrospective traffic decryption and build attribution. Not standard SIEM/EDR IOC types but included for analyst and threat hunter use.

xiebro_c2_aes_key.context: Hardcoded AES-ECB key used for ALL XiebroC2 C2 traffic (both directions). Recovered from DAT_00712b3a in main.exe binary. Keyboard-walk pattern indicates hand-typed key. Any PCAP of 193.56.255.154:4444 traffic can be decrypted with this key.

covenant_preshared_key_build1.context: Covenant pre-shared AES-256 key for GruntHTTP.exe (Build 1). Used in Phase 0 key exchange to encrypt RSA public key. Unique to this stager build.

covenant_preshared_key_build2.context: Covenant pre-shared AES-256 key for PS Embedded Stager (Build 2). Different from Build 1 — deliberate compartmentalization so compromise of one build does not affect the other.

build_artifacts.note: Operator build artifacts enable attribution and cross-build correlation. Present as embedded strings in binary/script.

covenant_session_token.context: Covenant listener-level POST session= token — shared across both GruntStager builds. Appears in every POST from every host infected by either stager. Highest-value network detection string for this campaign.

covenant_build_id.context: Covenant listener-level POST i= parameter — shared across both builds. Fixed 32-char hex build identifier.

covenant_guid_prefix_build1.context: Covenant GUID prefix unique to GruntHTTP.exe (Build 1). All victim sessions from this build share this prefix in their session GUID.

covenant_guid_prefix_build2.context: Covenant GUID prefix unique to PS Embedded Stager (Build 2).

xiebro_compile_path.context: Operator compile path embedded in main.exe pclntab (Go symbol table). Indicates ad-hoc compilation from operator Desktop with username admin.

xiebro_campaign_tag.context: Hardcoded campaign tag in main.exe (DAT_0096a980). Appears in all XiebroC2 response packets as server label.

xiebro_version_typo_string.context: XiebroC2 3.1 source code typo (missing second w in Windows) preserved in pclntab. Unique YARA static detection string for this family version.
